creator: imp@FreeBSD.org
text:
NANDFS had heap big time issues, not least was that the groomer was moved into the kernel. This normally wouldn't be terrible, but here there were two problems: locking was all screwed up by it (or was just implemented badly) and it always groomed, even when there was no new traffic, so the groomer slowly would rewrite the NAND over and over and over again. I wore out a MLC part while playing with it and tracked the issue down to this... That was in the FreeBSD 8 or 9 time frame and by FreeBSD 10 it wasn't working at all (even with this patch) and had all kinds of lock order issues because the locking protocols evolved substantially over time, but no changes were made here.

It's kind of a bummer, but doing a good nandfs takes a lot of time and effort and you have to keep up all the time on the latest nand parts / soc offload engines / etc. And we had none of that.
